channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: How an island became ferret free - thanks, in part, to Woody the wonderdog
item.description: In a world-first, Rathlin Island, off the coast of County Antrim, has eradicated ferrets.

item.title: Jess Hull says 'my time is coming' after another world championship medal
item.description: Jess Hull took women's silver and Adam Spencer nabbed men's bronze in the 1,500m at the World Athletics Indoor Championships.

item.title: Our son loved being outdoors - now ME means he can't walk or talk
item.description: It is heart-breaking for Myfanwy and Charlie Sleep to watch their once active 28-year-old son lying in bed every day, unable to walk or talk.

item.title: How a 15-year-old boy became a surprise Melbourne Cup winner
item.description: In 1948, country Victoria's Ray Neville stunned the nation, becoming one of the youngest jockeys to win the Melbourne Cup.

item.title: Prince William keen to build 'strong and meaningful' bond with Church
item.description: The Prince of Wales is "keen to build a strong and meaningful bond" with the Church of England, a royal aide has said, ahead of the future monarch attending the new Archbishop of Canterbury's official installation this week.

item.title: US high school student Cooper Lutkenhaus wins 800m to become youngest ever indoor world champion
item.description: US teenager Cooper Lutkenhaus made history on Sunday when he won gold in the 800m to becomes the youngest ever champion at the world indoor athletics championship.

item.title: Barking dogs to shared fences: How to resolve issues with your neighbour
item.description: What often starts as an ordinary issue with a neighbour, like a barking dog or overhanging tree, can snowball into conflict when not handled well.

item.title: Manchester City win Carabao Cup after Nico O’Reilly’s double downs lacklustre Arsenal
item.description: There was a time when the old trophy with the three handles felt like the personal property of Pep Guardiola.

item.title: Matildas need a grand plan to stave off end-of-an-era concerns
item.description: The day after the Matildas' loss to Japan in the final of the Women's Asian Cup, they fronted a 'thank you' event in Sydney, attended by a couple of thousand fans.

item.title: Ollie Watkins puts seal on Villa’s win over West Ham after England omission
item.description: While Ollie Watkins gave the most articulate response to his omission from the England squad with the second-half goal that consolidated Aston Villa’s place in the Premier League’s top four, the most rousing noise around Villa Park arrived when Youri Tielemans replaced John McGinn a minute earlier.

item.title: The Matildas’ near misses sting but their Asian Cup final suggests this great team are not done
item.description: Two steps to the left.

item.title: The kindness of strangers: My kids sent me a birthday fax from far away – a modest man made sure I received it
item.description: In the 1990s I travelled extensively for work, leaving behind my young family.

item.title: Starmer adviser urges ministers to look at profits cap for energy and petrol firms
item.description: The government’s top cost of living adviser has called on ministers to explore a temporary cap on the profits of energy and petrol companies to prevent them from cashing in excessively on the war in the Middle East.

item.title: How to make the perfect cheese khachapuri – recipe | Felicity Cloake's How to make the perfect …
item.description: Tuskadze goes on to explain in her book Supra that there are “as many variations … as there are families in Georgia” – the boat-shaped, open adjaruili that Polina Chesnakova notes has “taken the internet by storm”, the Ossetian mashed potato variety and the Gurian take with hard-boiled eggs and a “supremely fluffy, slightly oniony, soufflé-like cheese filling”, which inspires Caroline Eden to share with readers of her book Green Mountains the glorious Georgian word shemomechama, “which loosely translates as, ‘I accidentally ate the whole thing’”.

item.title: An orthopedic surgeon explains the hand injury that has many MLB players on the bench
item.description: Baseball hitters are on a quest for power.

item.title: ‘I want my career, my children and a free supple life’: Sylvia Plath’s radical reinvention
item.description: In February 1962, Sylvia Plath dropped in on her neighbour in Devon, Rose Key, with “a plate of absolutely indigestible Black Walnut-flavored cupcakes”.

item.title: Goggia wins to lock up World Cup super-G title. Aicher further closes gap on overall leader Shiffrin
item.description: KVITFJELL, Norway (AP) — Sofia Goggia won her third super-G of the season at the World Cup finals on Sunday to secure the discipline title, while Emma Aicher further closed the gap to overall leader Mikaela Shiffrin.

item.title: Welcome to allergy season. Here’s how to protect yourself
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Allergy season can be miserable for tens of millions of Americans when trees, grass, and other pollens cause runny noses, itchy eyes, coughing and sneezing.

item.title: Cooking with Angela Hartnett: ‘I love food, but I don’t need to talk about it 24/7’
item.description: Angela Hartnett’s home kitchen isn’t a place you could recreate, however much Le Creuset you bought.

item.title: How tacking centre left will help Labour win the next election | Andy Beckett
item.description: Led by a probably doomed prime minister, presiding over a struggling economy, exposed by an ongoing scandal, besieged by populist insurgents to its right and left, ambushed by a war that will bring higher inflation and public debt, and predicted to win just 75 seats at the next general election, according to the website Electoral Calculus, Labour is in an unprecedented crisis.

item.title: ‘We can create hype’: H&M’s UK boss on its commitment to the high street
item.description: Almost exactly 50 years after H&M opened its first British store, the doors on its newest, in Brighton, swung open this week and the Swedish fashion company’s UK boss is determined to keep investing in the nation’s high streets despite its struggles.

item.title: Slop it like it’s hot: the rise of build-your-own takeaway salad bowls
item.description: How did this pick-n-mix salad-and-protein sludge become a typical working lunch?.

item.title: Scrambling, walking and swimming in splendid isolation: 75 years of the UK’s national parks
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Breaking: One Nation wins first seat in SA Parliament's Lower House, ABC projects
item.description: Pauline Hanson's One Nation has won its first ever Lower House seat at an election outside of Queensland, the ABC projects.

item.title: It’s always me who makes the effort to see my friends. Don’t they value me? | Ask Annalisa Barbieri
item.description: My friends seem genuinely happy to see me when we’re together and usually accept when I suggest meeting up.

item.title: We are living in a period of political anti-intellectualism. But in pop culture, clever is the new cool
item.description: Put down your negroni, hang up your Prada handbag and pick up a paperback.

item.title: Family-friendly TV drama Crookhaven hopes to be latest hit book adaptation
item.description: There's nothing hotter in the world of TV right now than adaptations of popular books, especially those that cater to younger audiences.

item.title: Anyone who goes through IVF 'should be so proud', says Sara Pascoe
item.description: Sara Pascoe has said anyone who tries to conceive through IVF "should be so proud of themselves", explaining that it is "such a big thing to put your body through".

item.title: ‘So awesome’: BTS fans on K-pop’s biggest comeback
item.description: The Zubillaga family had come from Veracruz, Mexico.

item.title: Jess Hull and Kurtis Marschall win bronze as Australia eye more medals on final day at world indoors
item.description: Jess Hull and Kurtis Marschall have won bronze medals at the world athletics indoor championships on another successful day for the Australian team in Poland.

item.title: Is your jerry can safe and legal?
item.description: Despite repeated calls from authorities not to stockpile fuel, warning it will only push prices even higher as demand outpaces supply, some Australians are continuing to fill jerry cans and store petrol at home.

item.title: Hull, Marschall win bronze at World Athletics Indoor Championships
item.description: Jessica Hull won bronze in the women's 3,000 metres at the World Athletics Indoor Championships.

item.title: Three sailors remain missing near Iran. Their families are hoping for a 'miracle'
item.description: Three sailors remain missing after a Thai merchant ship was bombed by Iran as it tried to escape the war zone via the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Superannuation should be used for aged care, not inherited by next generation, aged care CEO says
item.description: Labor should more actively encourage wealthier Australians to spend more of their superannuation on their own care, an industry leader says, to help free up capacity in the struggling system to protect elderly people without means.

item.title: I lost my love of cooking after 12 years as a chef. Moving to a pig farm restored it
item.description: I was a keen-bean 15-year-old when I got my first job in a commercial kitchen in Canberra, raised on a diet of Jamie and Nigella and bursting with a passion for food.

item.title: Iraqi Kurds mark Nowruz, celebrating light over darkness
item.description: AKRE, Kurdistan Region of Iraq — For many people, the vernal equinox — which marked the beginning of spring Friday — is just another date on the calendar.

item.title: Fans elated as BTS play first gig together in nearly four years
item.description: Fans have poured into the historic heart of South Korea's capital for what may well be the year's biggest event: the return of the world's most successful band in recent years, BTS.

item.title: ‘I’m going to leave you some landmines’: Pauline Hanson sends SA premier a message amid jubilant supporters
item.description: “Today is not about Pauline Hanson,” Pauline Hanson told the crowd, as she took the microphone to chants of “Pauline! Pauline!”.

item.title: SA state election 2026: Labor secures victory as results show One Nation taking huge chunks of Liberal vote
item.description: Peter Malinauskas is on track for a landslide victory in South Australia’s state election, with Labor easily securing a second term and an increased majority in Saturday’s poll, amid a surge in support for One Nation.

item.title: ‘I was relieved I got the shot without my phone ending up in the sea!’: Easelyn Pineda’s best phone picture
item.description: Pineda waded into the water with her phone to get closer, later making adjustments to ensure the forms of the fishers weren’t lost in silhouette.

item.title: Best thing I ever ate? My first In-N-Out burger in LA
item.description: They say you never forget your first time, but for most of us, this doesn’t apply to cheeseburgers.

item.title: Carol Vorderman: ‘Best kiss of my life? There’s a long list … ’
item.description: Born in Bedfordshire, Carol Vorderman, 65, studied engineering at Cambridge University.

item.title: ‘Tastes of salt, smells of coffee’: why Trieste is one of Italy’s best food cities
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Live: Massive crowd as Matildas take on Japan in blockbuster Asian Cup final
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup final is upon us, with a red-hot Japan standing in the way of the Matildas and what could be their first piece of silverware since 2010.

item.title: Comic Relief helps fund free school uniform charity
item.description: As children across the country ditch school uniform for the day to raise money for Comic Relief, one Greater Manchester charity that has helped thousands of families with recycled uniforms will be receiving funding.

item.title: Becoming a train driver in my 40s was the best thing I ever did
item.description: "I absolutely love it, I wish I'd done it years ago," Sharon Paton says, of her decision to change careers to become a train driver in her 40s.

item.title: Labor's landslide win, as Liberals fall and One Nation rises
item.description: Labor has secured victory in South Australia with Peter Malinauskas winning a second term.

item.title: Love Actually? Washington’s current relationship with Britain is more like Contempt Actually | Timothy Garton Ash
item.description: “A friend who bullies us is no longer a friend.

item.title: Rhoda Roberts, the woman behind 'Welcome to Country', dies at 66
item.description: A guiding force in Australia's arts scene and the woman who coined the term "Welcome to Country", Aunty Rhoda Roberts AO has died at the age of 66.

item.title: ‘A fascinating discovery’: research challenges Battle of Hastings narrative
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Dockers much too strong for Dees, Petracca hurt in Suns' win over Tigers
item.description: Fremantle has overpower Melbourne for a 48-point win, thrilling the home crowd as the Dockers got off the mark in 2026.

item.title: Saturday Night Live UK is here - but can it make you laugh?
item.description: Walking into the Saturday Night Live UK studios for the first time, it's clear much time and money has been pumped into helping this show succeed.

item.title: My love of baking helps me deal with arthritis at age of 13
item.description: After being diagnosed with juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) in 2024, she found it became therapeutic way of coping with a condition that many people she knew had never even heard of.

item.title: Maguire red card costs Manchester United as Kroupi rescues Bournemouth draw
item.description: Under Friday night lights, Manchester United stumbled.

item.title: It's not a royal visit, but something else important happened in Tassie this week
item.description: You'd have to be living under a rock if you missed that "our" Danish Queen Mary visited Hobart this week.

item.title: Small but mighty cheers for team Japan at Asian Cup final
item.description: Young Japanese diaspora in Sydney will be showing up loud and proud in support of Japan against Australia's Matildas at the Asian Cup final on Saturday.

item.title: Polls predict easy Labor win in SA, but One Nation will be closely watched
item.description: One Nation isn't shying away from the national significance of today.

item.title: Top US Fema official claims to have teleported to a Waffle House before
item.description: A far-right conspiracy theorist turned high-ranking official at the US Federal Emergency Management Agency (Fema) claims to have once teleported to a Waffle House.

item.title: Starmer most popular PM - in Toby Jug sales
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer may have had a tough year in office, but in one key measure he is top of the shop: the Toby Jug in his likeness outsold all other prime ministers in 2025.

item.title: Why Ryan Gosling's eyes were so important to Project Hail Mary
item.description: Australian cinematographer Grieg Fraser has recreated pretty much every earthly environment you can think of; from the night vision deserts of Zero Dark Thirty, to the comic-book grime of Gotham City in The Batman to queasy sheen of Washington politics in Vice.

item.title: Work from home and drive more slowly to save energy, global body urges
item.description: With energy prices still high due to the conflict in the Gulf, governments around the world are being urged to reduce driving speed limits and encourage more working from home.

item.title: ‘We need to think much bigger’: trade minister calls for greater ambition in UK-EU reset
item.description: It was all smiles and warm handshakes when the two men in charge of renegotiating the UK’s relationship with the EU met in Brussels this week.

item.title: Jehovah’s Witnesses ease policy on transfusions, allowing storage and use of one’s own blood
item.description: Leaders of Jehovah’s Witnesses are modifying their prohibition on receiving blood transfusions on religious grounds, now allowing members to decide whether to allow their own blood to be drawn and stored in advance for such things as a scheduled surgery with a risk of significant blood loss.

item.title: Many states count mail ballots that arrive after Election Day. Those grace periods could go away
item.description: There will be just one Election Day for this fall’s midterm elections — Nov.

item.title: ‘We need to believe’: Matildas embrace underdogs tag for last shot at home glory in Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: The end is nigh, and Ellie Carpenter – even though she is not 26 for another month – knows it.

item.title: What’s the best solution to the world’s current problems? | Fiona Katauskas
item.description: Implementing it may be a challenge, howeverSee more of Fiona Katauskas’s cartoons here Continue reading.

item.title: South Australian election: Labor heads for a win, the Liberals a battering and One Nation a test of surging support
item.description: In an odd campaign Peter Malinauskas mocks Pauline Hanson’s anti-immigration stance and she finger-wags back.

item.title: The best bike lights in the US to see and be seen
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Matisse, 1941-1954 review – hit after glorious hit in a show of life-enhancing genius
item.description: Forget the joy and energy of youth – your best days might yet be ahead.

item.title: Cocktail of the week: Bear by Carlo Scotto’s wild garlic martini – recipe | The good mixer
item.description: It’s wild garlic season, which is as much cause for celebration on the drinks trolley as it is in the kitchen.

item.title: Greg James completes Comic Relief challenge, raising more than £4m
item.description: BBC Radio 1's Greg James has crossed the finish line of his Comic Relief tandem cycling challenge, raising more than £4m for charity.

item.title: ‘Weirdness, paranoia and extremity’: why HBO’s Neighbors is TV’s most fascinating show
item.description: Once upon a time, I worked as a local reporter in small-town Montana.

item.title: Live: Bulldog Baker's brilliant goal seals thrilling win over fast-finishing Crows
item.description: The Western Bulldogs have hung on to claim a thrilling six-point win over Adelaide, with a brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinching the win as the Crows stormed home.

item.title: Giddey sets new Aussie NBA record as Bulls fall short of miracle comeback
item.description: Josh Giddey has recorded a career-high 19 assists in a loss to Cleveland, the most by an Australian in a single NBA game.

item.title: Brisbane pull off grand final repeat in rematch with Storm
item.description: Brisbane have inflicted another second-half nightmare on the Storm, roaring back to win their grand final replay 18-14 in Melbourne to secure their first points of the NRL season.

item.title: More than 1,600 new gas wells approved for rural Queensland until 2081
item.description: The federal government has approved more than 1,600 new gas wells for rural Queensland until 2081.

item.title: Social supermarket praise for Comic Relief support
item.description: The former teaching assistant and her husband found themselves out of work because of ill health and were on benefits.

item.title: Helen Goh’s recipe for peanut and blackcurrant thumbprint cookies | The sweet spot
item.description: Niki Segnit writes in The Flavour Thesaurus that, while grape jelly is the familiar partner to peanut butter in the classic PBJ, she thinks blackcurrant, with its sharper, more complex character, would be a far better match for the fatty and salty peanuts.

item.title: Adorable alert! A Tasmanian possum has fallen asleep among the soft toys in an airport gift shop | First Dog on the Moon
item.description: The little guy just needed somewhere to napSign up here to get an email whenever First Dog cartoons are publishedGet all your needs met at the First Dog shop if what you need is First Dog merchandise and prints Continue reading.

item.title: 'Asking why honey is better than s***': Japan coach spins favouritism ahead of Matildas clash
item.description: It's an unusual question, posed by one of the most fascinating coaches at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: A bust of Barbra Streisand and beautiful memories: Richard E Grant’s garden – in seven extraordinary items
item.description: Step into Richard E Grant’s garden in Richmond, London, and you’ll be met with a rather unconventional sight.

item.title: Luka Dončić erupts for 60 as surging LA Lakers extend win streak in Miami
item.description: Luka Dončić scored 60 points, LeBron James had a triple-double on a night where he tied the NBA record for games played, and the Los Angeles Lakers pushed their season-best winning streak to eight games with a 134-126 win over the Miami Heat on Thursday night.

item.title: Teen happiness falls in western countries as social media hours rise
item.description: Costa Rica breaks into the World Happiness Report's top five, while Nordic countries stay on top and Australia stays outside the top 10.

item.title: Australia dig themselves out of a hole in women’s T20 win over West Indies
item.description: Australia got the Sophie Molineux era under way in sloppy style as a new-look side overcame six spilled chances before turning the screw late for a 43-run victory over West Indies in the first women’s T20 international in St Vincent.

item.title: Teen becomes youngest player since Nadal to win ATP Masters 1000 match
item.description: French tennis player Moise Kouame has become the youngest player to win a match at the Miami Open.

item.title: 'The ABC got lucky': How the VFA became must-watch TV for footy fans
item.description: Phil Cleary, Sam Kekovich, Peter Gee, Len Thompson and Ross Booth together in a promo shot for the ABC's VFA coverage.

item.title: Traitors, Amandaland - and Idris Elba - get set for Comic Relief
item.description: Marcella star Anna Friel is to play Claudia Winkleman in a sketch for this year's Comic Relief, alongside some recent stars of The Traitors such as Rachel Duffy and Faraaz Noor.

item.title: UK's most popular tourist attraction for 2025 takes top spot from British Museum
item.description: The Natural History Museum was the UK's most popular tourist attraction in 2025, breaking the record for the most visitors to any museum or gallery in a single year.

item.title: Natural History Museum tops UK attraction list with record visitors
item.description: London’s Natural History Museum (NHM) was the most popular attraction in the UK during 2025, with its renovated gardens, new climate gallery and lack of entry fee leading to record-breaking numbers of visitors.

item.title: High Point’s upset of Wisconsin ends the hope of a perfect March Madness bracket for millions
item.description: Well, the dream was fun while it lasted .

item.title: Xavier Edmonds’ layup with 4.3 seconds left lifts TCU to 66-64 March Madness win over Ohio State
item.description: GREENVILLE, S.C.

item.title: Concerns over super balances as around $250b wiped off Australian share market
item.description: The war in the Middle East is escalating with more attacks on critical energy infrastructure.

item.title: Huge queues for jabs as meningitis vaccination scheme expanded
item.description: A meningitis vaccination programme has been expanded following a deadly outbreak in Kent - with university students queuing for hours for jabs.

item.title: Afroman keeps trolling cops after winning “Lemon Pound Cake” defamation case
item.description: Cops asked the jury for millions after Afroman used raid footage in music videos.

item.title: Premier League set to amend spending rules to give clubs more flexibility for transfers
item.description: The Premier League is poised to amend its new spending rules before they are introduced next season to give clubs greater flexibility in the transfer market.

item.title: The best pressure washers in the UK for cleaning garden furniture and patios – tested
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: ‘My taste is superb. My eyes are exquisite’: Dianne Wiest’s 20 best film performances – ranked!
item.description: Every great performer should have at least one baffling movie on their CV, and this curio, produced by Ismail Merchant, is Dianne Wiest’s.

item.title: Príncipe Discos: how Black DJs from Lisbon’s suburbs made Europe’s most exciting record label
item.description: It’s just after 11.30pm on a Friday in early March, and the air at Lux Frágil is already thick with excitement.

item.title: Record crowds, empty seats and the Matildas in a dream final: has the Women’s Asian Cup been a success?
item.description: Sarah Walsh has heard the naysayers, heard the voices sniggering about half-empty stadiums and unsold tickets.

item.title: Show us your dome! Hot, balding, confident men are bringing sexy back | Rebecca Shaw
item.description: The Oscars were this week, and as usual I took the day off to judge the red carpet looks from my tracksuit pants-ed position on the couch, pick apart the monologue (love you Conan), declare I could write better presenter segments, and keep an eye on social media to see which stars cause the most buzz.

item.title: Striking gambit: Erling Haaland invests in new world chess championship
item.description: Erling Haaland has become a significant investor in a new world chess championship tour that is expected to star his fellow Norwegian Magnus Carlsen, the Guardian can reveal.

item.title: RFK Jr. makes food sound like a miracle drug. Researchers say he often overstates the science
item.description: In the Trump administration’s campaign to promote healthy eating, Robert F.

item.title: The housing charity helping key workers stay local
item.description: A housing organisation that offers key workers affordable rental properties has celebrated 20 years since it was founded.

item.title: Underscores: U review – ultra-imaginative auteur has pop’s most brilliant brain
item.description: April Grey is a US bedroom producer beloved of an impressive range of other artists – experimental pop duo 100 Gecs are fans, so is rapper Danny Brown and Blink-182 drummer Travis Barker – but thus far it’s been hard to accurately pin her down.

item.title: Nightclub linked to outbreak 'won't open until people are safe' says owner
item.description: The owner of a nightclub linked to the centre of a deadly meningitis outbreak in Kent says it "will not open until this is under control" after two members of staff were hospitalised.

item.title: Kick your tiredness with these 7 natural energy boosters
item.description: Feeling like a wilted flower most days? Perk up with these natural energy boosters.

item.title: César Chavez and Dolores Huerta led a movement that won better wages and conditions for farmworkers
item.description: Dolores Huerta and the late César Chavez are both labor rights icons credited with leading a movement that pushed growers to negotiate for better wages and working conditions for farmworkers.

item.title: Former DHS official on how the agency's next leader can be successful in the role
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former DHS official Miles Taylor about the confirmation hearing for Sen.

item.title: Mum 'so proud' of deafblind son's Eastenders debut
item.description: A mum whose five-year-old deafblind son has made his acting debut in Eastenders has said she is "so proud" of him and she hopes it will "break down barriers" for disabled children.

item.title: Live: Great Gunston gets Hawks home at the MCG
item.description: Hawthorn finishes over the top of an understrength Sydney side to knock off the ladder-leaders at the MCG, thanks to a four-goal haul from Jack Gunston.

item.title: Better NHS care might have saved 58 babies, BBC finds
item.description: At least 58 babies at an NHS maternity unit might have survived with better care, a BBC investigation has found.

item.title: Aurora australis might soon be on display. Here's how to get the best view
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Mare by Emily Haworth-Booth review – profound story of a woman’s love for a horse
item.description: Mare, Emily Haworth-Booth’s wonderful first novel for adults, is about a woman confronting three life-altering crises.

item.title: We need to be honest about Iran – and how our rampant greed for oil is causing mayhem | George Monbiot
item.description: I realise this is a serious breach of etiquette.

item.title: Night buses will run to every Greater Manchester borough as Bee Network expands
item.description: Night buses will run to every borough in Greater Manchester as the city region expands its publicly controlled Bee Network.

item.title: Last One Laughing UK review – the funniest TV show of the year
item.description: It could easily have been a fluke.

item.title: Lizzy Hoo's surprising love of Mongolian indie rock
item.description: Australian comedian Lizzy Hoo loves an adventure, a trait she reckons she inherited from her mum, who left Toowoomba to go backpacking in the 70s.

item.title: Ryan Gosling on bringing humour to sci-fi adventure Project Hail Mary
item.description: Humour and science fiction may not seem obvious bedfellows but a history of cinema will tell you different.

item.title: Check mates: analysis of medieval chess sets reveal vision of equality and mutual respect
item.description: On the chessboard, black and white pieces are lined up against each other for an unrelenting battle.

item.title: 'One of the greatest': Influential former judge dies weeks shy of 101st birthday
item.description: Sir Anthony Mason, who presided as chief judge of the High Court between 1987 and 1995, has died aged 100.

item.title: Liverpool v Galatasaray, Spurs v Atlético Madrid and more: Champions League last-16 second legs – live
item.description: Spurs appeal for a foul on Simons in the buildup by the goalscorer … but the goal stands.

item.title: Live: EU president to visit Australia as sides inch closer to signing free trade deal
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen will visit Australia next week to meet with the prime minister as Australia inches closer to signing a free trade agreement with Europe.

item.title: UConn teammates Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd headline AP All-America first team
item.description: Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd helped UConn to an undefeated season heading into March Madness.

item.title: I didn’t know how much I needed work until I lost it. But now I’ve learned to love Mondays again | Adrian Chiles
item.description: I do like Mondays.

item.title: Famous Iditarod dog sled race ends in repeat win for Jessie Holmes
item.description: Jessie Holmes has won his second consecutive Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race in Nome, Alaska.

item.title: Mamdani put Ramadan at the center of NYC's cultural life, bringing joy -- and a backlash
item.description: When Mayor Zohran Mamdani took the stage at the Museum of the City of New York last week, he was surrounded by city workers invited to share iftar, the dinner held after sunset to break the daily Ramadan fast.

item.title: Farage called for release of Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs and praised effort to free drug trafficker
item.description: Nigel Farage called for the release of the imprisoned rapper Sean “Diddy” Combs and commended the efforts to free a former Honduran president jailed in the US for drug trafficking.

item.title: ‘Prince laughed like a kid as I painted “Free” on his stomach’: Steve Parke’s best photograph
item.description: I grew up loving Prince’s music and remember thinking: “I’m gonna work for that guy one day.” Through high school and college I photographed local bands.

item.title: Greetings from Nyeri, Kenya, where grandmothers help coach the next generation
item.description: Far-Flung Postcards is a weekly series in which NPR's international team shares moments from their lives and work around the world.

item.title: The BTS comeback album is almost here – and you best believe I’m slipping back into my K-pop obsessive era | Aastha Agrawal
item.description: When I heard the surprise news that BTS are releasing a comeback album, it pulled me back into a past version of myself, one that was all-consuming in its obsession, but equally marked by shame.

item.title: Jess Cartner-Morley on fashion: primary colours are back, but styling them isn’t child’s play
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: The one who got away: how did Australia lose one of its brightest football talents to Croatia? | Jack Snape
item.description: There have been many to choose other nations over Australia, but for Socceroos fans this one hurts.

item.title: Other nations danced for joy at the World Baseball Classic. Team USA played toy soldiers
item.description: On the morning of the World Baseball Classic final between the United States and Venezuela, the headline of the New York Times daily briefing read, “America, alone,” in reference to the unwillingness of the country’s traditional allies to join the war with Iran.

item.title: Record property profits see home owner wealth surge despite rising rates
item.description: For the first time in 15 years more than 90 per cent of house resales in all Australian capital cities have returned a profit, according to property portal Domain.

item.title: Urgent call to ensure all babies are born a healthy weight
item.description: The Productivity Commission's latest Closing the Gap data shows only four out of 19 targets are on track.

item.title: Top WNBA players set to make more than $1m a year after new CBA agreed in principle
item.description: The WNBA and its players’ union reached an agreement in principle on a new collective bargaining agreement early Wednesday morning.

item.title: We asked experts about the most responsible ways to use AI tools – here’s what they said
item.description: Three years on from the release of ChatGPT, two broad camps have formed: those people who refuse to use it, and those who use it every day.

item.title: The best cordless vacuum cleaners for a spotless home: 10 tried and tested favourites
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Woman who learned to walk again after 130ft fall meets 'incredible' rescuers
item.description: A woman who had to learn to walk again after a 130ft (39.6m) fall at a beauty spot has been reunited with the teams who rescued her, saying she will be "forever grateful" to them for saving her life.

item.title: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton wins Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate
item.description: Among the crowded field of Democratic primary candidates, Juliana Stratton said she would strongly oppose the Trump administration and not vote for any of his appointees, if elected.

item.title: 'Special human' James Slipper to break Super Rugby record
item.description: Brumbies prop James Slipper will soon have played more Super Rugby games than anyone else.

item.title: Live: The Asian Cup's two best teams duel to win right to face Matildas in final
item.description: Japan has been rampant at the tournament so far, scoring 17 group stage goals before easily accounting for the Philippines in the quarterfinals.

item.title: 'Moment of pride': Venezuela edges US to win first World Baseball Classic
item.description: Venezuela has claimed its first World Baseball Classic championship with a 3-2 win over the United States in Miami.

item.title: A moment that changed me: I applied mucous-tinted mascara – and loved the reaction
item.description: I wore makeup for the first time just after I turned 12: a tube of green mascara from a pound shop in my home town in south Wales.

item.title: Venezuela beats U.S. 3-2, wins first World Baseball Classic title
item.description: MIAMI — Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, rebounding from a blown eighth-inning lead to beat the United States 3-2 Tuesday night on Eugenio Suárez's tiebreaking double in the ninth.

item.title: ‘Old masters too’: Ghent exhibition celebrates female artists of the baroque
item.description: Judith Leyster, an artist of the Dutch golden age, was thought to be about 21 when she painted her self-portrait in 1630.

item.title: Shaun Ryder on highs, lows and Happy Mondays: ‘Heroin isn’t a party drug – you can’t just do it at the weekend’
item.description: There are thousands of pictures of Shaun Ryder and Bez in Happy Mondays, from the mid- to late 80s, that run the gamut from mashed to wrecked.

item.title: Venezuela win first World Baseball Classic title after taming USA in politically fraught final
item.description: Venezuela defeated the United States 3-2 on Tuesday night to win their first World Baseball Classic title, a landmark triumph in a politically charged final that resonated far beyond the diamond.

item.title: Juliana Stratton wins Illinois Democratic Senate primary race
item.description: Illinois lieutenant governor Juliana Stratton won the Democratic primary race to succeed Illinois’ US senator Dick Durbin in Washington, beating out US representative Raja Krishnamoorthi.

item.title: Why the B-52s are trading their Love Shack for Downton Abbey
item.description: If you see a faded sign at the side of the road that says, "15 miles to the Love Shack," you know you're in for a good time.

item.title: Daniel Biss wins Democratic primary for closely-watched Illinois House seat
item.description: Daniel Biss, the mayor of Evanston, Ill., has won the Democratic primary for the state's 9th congressional district, according to a race call by The Associated Press, topping a crowded field in the race for a Chicago-area seat that has not been open for nearly three decades.

item.title: Lucky escape for mine workers almost hit by debris during explosive blasts
item.description: Workers were almost hit by debris, known as "flyrock", in two separate explosive incidents at WA mine sites in recent months.

item.title: Rangers combine ancient wisdom with modern technology to care for country
item.description: The first Aboriginal rangers conference for the NSW South Coast has been held in Mystery Bay.

item.title: Eze keeps Arsenal’s shot at history alive with magic moment against Leverkusen | Barney Ronay
item.description: And now I am become Springtime Eze, destroyer of netting.

item.title: Senegal's AFCON win overturned months after controversial final
item.description: Morocco has been awarded the Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) title by appeals judges who stunningly overturned Senegal's victory in a chaotic final in January.

item.title: Adelaide 36ers into their first NBL grand final since 2018
item.description: The Adelaide 36ers have secured their first NBL grand final berth since 2018.

item.title: Matildas coach explains Raso absence in Asian Cup semifinal win over China
item.description: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro has revealed his decision not to play Hayley Raso in the 2-1 semifinal win over China was purely tactical, declaring the star winger fit to go for Saturday's decider.

item.title: Live: Chance of RBA interest rate hike in May drops to 40pc, ASX likely to rise
item.description: The likelihood of a follow-up interest rate hike in May may has fallen substantially after the Reserve Bank's 5-4 vote in favour of yesterday's rate increase — the slimmest of majorities.

item.title: Sporting’s stunning turnaround ends Bodø/Glimt’s Champions League run
item.description: Sporting staged a stunning comeback to beat Bodø/Glimt 5-0 after extra time and reach the Champions League quarter-finals with a 5-3 aggregate victory in their last-16 tie.

item.title: Manchester City v Real Madrid: Champions League last 16, second leg – live
item.description: 50 min Doku has played well tonight, picking really clever passes, and with Alexander-Arnold expecting to be run it, instead he punches a square pass to Haaland who shoots … and Lunin parries really well, the rebound falling back to the Norwegian Les Dawson … who shoots over.

item.title: Reform UK may breach data laws with free energy bills competition
item.description: Reform UK risks breaching data protection laws with its competition to win free energy bills for a year, lawyers and data experts have warned.

item.title: Sam Kerr’s moment of magic sends Matildas into Women’s Asian Cup final with 2-1 win over China
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Report finds 'significant and sustained shift' in support for salmon industry
item.description: A report by Australia's national science agency has found Tasmania's salmon farming industry has experienced a "significant and sustained shift" away from strong community support it experienced a decade ago.

item.title: 'You have to laugh': Why this critique of corporate work culture is a comedy
item.description: Do Not Pass Go is a play set entirely in one room of a workplace, but we never find out what the work actually is.

item.title: Mayors to gain more spending power under Reeves tax plans
item.description: Regional mayors could be given control of some tax revenue to spend on their local priorities, Chancellor Rachel Reeves has announced.

item.title: Switch 2's new "Handheld Mode Boost" can run original Switch games at 1080p
item.description: Switch games running at 720p can look worse on the Switch 2’s 1080p display.

item.title: Prince William joins Greg James on Comic Relief bike ride
item.description: The Prince of Wales surprised Radio 1's Greg James by joining the presenter during his 630-mile tandem bike ride challenge.

item.title: Honey as a superfood: can it really heal wounds, fight superbugs and provide sweet relief for coughs? | Antiviral
item.description: Humans have been consuming honey for thousands of years.

item.title: Aspiring female Zambian politicians asked for sexual favours, official says
item.description: A senior Zambian government official has said that some political parties were asking prospective female candidates for sexual favours in exchange for selection ahead of August's general election.

item.title: Rachel Reeves to call for rapid AI adoption and deeper ties with EU to boost growth – business live
item.description: Good morning, and welcome to our rolling coverage of business, the financial markets and the world economy.

item.title: Free energy packs 'to reduce bills by up to £800'
item.description: A charity is giving away packs which they claim could reduce energy bills by up to £800 per year.

item.title: The Breakdown | From Ramos to Carré: selecting the best XV of the 2026 Six Nations
David Squires on … Max Dowman, Arsenal’s great release and Chelsea’s Tierney totem
item.description: Our cartoonist on the Gunners’ teenage saviour and a new springtime ritual at Stamford BridgeBuy a cartoon | David’s favourite works of 2025And his latest book, Chaos in the Box: get it now Continue reading.

item.title: Is there a more fair way to sell World Cup tickets?
item.description: Last October, I had a decision to make.

item.title: Why we fell in love with Love Story: JFK Jr and Carolyn Bessette
item.description: On a recent sunny Sunday in New York, Love Story seemed to be everywhere.

item.title: Could a stressed-out AI model help us win the battle against big tech? Let me ask Claude | Coco Khan
item.description: I am, in the way of my country, an over-apologiser.

item.title: Live: Midfield star returns for the Matildas' blockbuster Asian Cup semifinal
item.description: The semifinals of the Women's Asian Cup begin with the Matildas taking on defending champion and tournament titan China at Perth Stadium.

item.title: ‘We were all so happy’: Arsenal bid to harness feel-good factor against Leverkusen
item.description: Even the man who Mikel Arteta has described as his chief “chaos creator” almost found things a bit too much.

item.title: A total hoot! Beautiful birds – in pictures
item.description: From fluffy owlets to rosy-hued flamingos, Claire Rosen’s portraits of live birds took her on a journey that touched on colonialism, wallpaper design … and chickens Continue reading.

item.title: Are football Ultras a menace or a force for good?
item.description: Smoke bombs, pyrotechnics, balaclavas and punch-ups - welcome to the world of the football Ultras.

item.title: And the burger goes to... Michael B Jordan marks Oscars win at fast food chain
item.description: After he was handed a statuette for best actor at the Academy Awards, Michael B Jordan received another golden prize - a cheeseburger.

item.title: Australia news live: fresh blow to mortgage holders as RBA hikes official interest rate to 4.1% in second consecutive increase
item.description: Australia’s Reserve Bank had been the only one expected to hike so soon, with central banks in the US, UK, European Union, Japan, Canada, Switzerland and Sweden all expected to leave rates on hold this week.

item.title: 'We have exciting days ahead': Danish royals arrive in Melbourne
item.description: The king and queen of Denmark are in Melbourne for two days of royal engagements as part of their Australian state visit.

item.title: Train delays: compensation claims to be easier under Great British Railways
item.description: Rail passengers will be able to claim compensation for delayed trains directly from the website where they bought their ticket, the government has said, as part of a shake-up to make rail travel simpler.

item.title: Which Oscar-winning movie best reflects our reality? | Fiona Katauskas
item.description: Truth is sometimes stranger than fictionSee more of Fiona Katauskas’s cartoons here Continue reading.

item.title: ‘The Premier League is surprisingly lenient’: did Chelsea get off lightly?
item.description: The Premier League’s judgment against Chelsea for breaking football’s rules – during an eight-year trophy spree under Roman Abramovich – concludes that the club engaged in “deception and concealment”.

item.title: And the winner is ... all of us? How the Oscars have changed for the better
item.description: Last year, as the major fall film festivals took place around the world, it was hard to make out the sound of audience applause.

item.title: The Taylor Swift effect: US vinyl sales top $1bn for the first time since 1983
item.description: For the first time in over four decades, US vinyl sales have topped $1bn in annual revenue.

item.title: Girl in meningitis outbreak was 'lovely and caring'
item.description: A sixth former who died following an outbreak of meningitis in Kent was "genuinely caring" and had a "loving nature", her head teacher has said.

item.title: Aaron Judge hails World Baseball Classic crowds as ‘bigger and better than the World Series’
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — On a night when All-Stars and future Hall of Famers thrilled fans and each other until the final out, Aaron Judge proclaimed the World Baseball Classic had grown into the sport’s biggest event.

item.title: Norway ‘climbs out of shadow’ of neighbours with first Oscar win for Sentimental Value
item.description: Sentimental Value’s victory at the Oscars on Sunday has been hailed in Norway as the moment the country stepped out of the film industry shadow cast by its Scandinavian neighbours Sweden and Denmark.

item.title: Key Oscar moments: Paul Thomas Anderson and Amy Madigan wins, outstanding songs and sad goodbyes
item.description: It was a 3 hour and 40 minute whirl through cinema and celebration, with Michael B.

item.title: ‘They become part of people’s identity’: how Australia fell in love with the Skywhales
item.description: All around Australia, Patricia Piccinini has been approached by people who are rolling up a sleeve, tugging at a trouser leg, or even lifting their T-shirt.

item.title: ‘We did Disneyland on mind-altering substances’: Primus frontman Les Claypool on being rock’s great joker – and why Metallica rejected him
item.description: It was the first oddball creation of many: Primus’s rubbery fusions of prog, metal and funk have made Claypool one of rock’s most unlikely success stories.

item.title: Delight across Ireland at Jessie Buckley’s ‘historic’ best actress Oscar win
item.description: Jessie Buckley’s Oscar win has uncorked delight across Ireland and prompted an early start to St Patrick’s Day celebrations.

item.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Oz team
item.description: Two members of Iran's national women's football team who sought asylum in Australia have been pictured training at the grounds of the A-league football team Brisbane Roar.

item.title: Fàbregas outwits Gasperini to take controversial Como a step closer to Champions League | Nicky Bandini
item.description: For once the TV cameras at the Stadio Giuseppe Sinigaglia had not picked out a Hollywood A-lister in the stands but a celebrity of calcio instead.

item.title: Better than Wuthering Heights? The Brontës’ novels – ranked!
item.description: This was the first novel that Charlotte Brontë completed.

item.title: Crossword editor’s desk: the joy of The Goodies and a setter deviates from the letter of house-style law
item.description: … with the perfectly accurate observation that BOTSWANA is not in the country of South Africa.

item.title: Starmer announces £53m to help households most hit by rising heating oil costs
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has said the government will provide £53m to help "vulnerable" households who have been hit by a sharp increase in the price of heating oil.

item.title: How a charity founded by farmers' wives helps children in the Borders
item.description: A charity founded by farmers' wives in the 1960s to protect vulnerable children is celebrating its 60th birthday by appealing for more people to support it.

item.title: 'Goatsucker' bird makes 'remarkable comeback'
item.description: The number of nightjars recorded in a National Park is thought to have doubled in the past five years following conservation efforts.

item.title: A century after the first rocket launch, Ars staffers pick their favorites
item.description: Robert Goddard, a Massachusetts-born physicist, launched the world’s first liquid-fueled rocket on this date 100 years ago.

item.title: Why run for the love of it when you could be making butter at the same time | Emma Beddington
item.description: There is a new entry in the overstuffed “What fresh hell?” category for 2026 – people have started making butter while they run.

item.title: Far-left and far-right gains throw French mainstream parties into a quandary
item.description: The emerging strength of the far left and far right is creating awkward choices for mainstream parties after the first round of French mayoral elections.

item.title: Sinners’ Oscar triumphs show that Black cinema is now a vital and valid part of Hollywood
item.description: Congratulations to the Sinners camp on its Oscar night triumphs – affirmation that cinema can be deep and entertaining at the same time.

item.title: London book fair roundup: Idris Elba’s thriller deal, the rise of romcom, and fights against censorship
item.description: The annual London book fair wrapped on Thursday, marking the end of three days that saw 33,000 people connected to the book industry – agents, publishers, authors, among others – gather at Olympia to make deals and discuss the state of the publishing world, and its future.

item.title: Influencers push 'parasite cleanses' but doctors say to steer clear
item.description: In online posts and videos, they suggest the organisms can be blamed for a host of symptoms — bloating, insomnia, food cravings, teeth grinding, headaches, among others.

item.title: Thames Water lenders float new £10bn rescue plan
item.description: Thames Water’s lenders have put forward a £10bn rescue plan that would involve paying off the troubled water company’s hundreds of millions of pounds-worth of fines for leaks and pollution, as part of an effort to stave off financial collapse.

item.title: India hopeful Iran talks will ease Hormuz route for its ships
item.description: Indian Foreign Minister S Jaishankar has said he is hopeful that talks with Iran are beginning to ease shipping disruptions for the country's vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Cherry on the top: Jessie Buckley pulled off a stunning double Oscar win for herself … and Chanel
item.description: There are two ways to win at the Oscars: go home with a statuette, or be crowned on the red carpet.

item.title: Rukmini Iyer’s quick and easy reccipe for crispy baked gnocchi puttanesca | Quick and easy
item.description: I’ve non-canonically added in small cubes of aubergine, because they melt beautifully into the sauce while adding extra plant matter.

item.title: 'We don't talk about it enough': Mum makes emotional return to Super Netball
item.description: Super Netball's 10th season has been tipped to be its most unpredictable yet, so it was only fitting that we get some surprising results in round one.

item.title: Cillian Murphy opens up about Peaky Blinders: best podcasts of the week
item.description: The stars of the smash hit Birmingham-set series tell all to Edith Bowman.

item.title: Five of Europe’s best accessible island escapes
item.description: Connected to the German mainland by a single rail causeway, Sylt is just over three hours from Hamburg by direct train.

item.title: South Korea celebrates ‘miracle’ Oscar wins for KPop Demon Hunters
item.description: Two Oscar wins for hit film KPop Demon Hunters have been met with jubilation in South Korea, where the film has been hailed as a cultural “miracle” and a win for the country’s artistic exports.

item.title: Does a 'Great Firewall' loom? Why Russians are buying up walkie-talkies and road maps
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: After experiencing youth homelessness, Sully wants to help others
item.description: Youth homelessness is becoming such an issue in Perth that on one average night earlier this month, services identified 73 young people sleeping rough, but only three available beds in crisis services.

item.title: TikTok and Meta risked safety to win algorithm arms race, whistleblowers say
item.description: Social media giants made decisions which allowed more harmful content on people's feeds, after internal research into their algorithms showed how outrage fuelled engagement, whistleblowers told the BBC.

item.title: Petrol is running out! Is it time to panic?! Help help | First Dog on the Moon
item.description: The government is asking people to remain calm which is always a sign that it is time to flip out massivelySign up here to get an email whenever First Dog cartoons are publishedGet all your needs met at the First Dog shop if what you need is First Dog merchandise and prints Continue reading.

item.title: 5 takeaways from an Oscars night that spread the love
item.description: As Sunday's Oscars ceremony approached, it seemed to be shaping up to be a showdown between the vampires and the revolutionaries, between Sinners and One Battle After Another.

item.title: ‘Next year, it’s going to be a Waymo in a tux’: the best quotes from Oscars 2026
item.description: Hathaway: Anna, just curious, what do you think of my dress tonight?.

item.title: New rock monitor lizard species reveals remarkable evolutionary story
item.description: Three new species of rock monitor lizards previously unknown to science have been formally described in Far North Queensland.

item.title: Historic firsts, risqué jokes, and a tie - watch the top moments
item.description: The 2026 Oscars featured surprise wins, emotional speeches and some comical moments as stars gathered for Hollywood's biggest night.

item.title: One Battle After Another sweeps the Oscars as Michael B Jordan and Jessie Buckley win big
item.description: Paul Thomas Anderson’s counter-culture caper One Battle After Another has won the Oscars war, taking home six awards after a hotly contested season.

item.title: Best animated film winner Kpop Demon Hunters captures hearts world over
item.description: Kpop Demon Hunters has won best animated film at the Oscars on Sunday evening - the latest in a growing list of accolades for the movie that has swept the world since it was released last summer.

item.title: Sinners’ Autumn Durald Arkapaw becomes first woman – and first black person – to win best cinematography Oscar
item.description: Sinners’ director of photography Autumn Durald Arkapaw has become the first woman to win the Oscar for best cinematography at the 98th Academy Awards.

item.title: Mr Nobody Against Putin wins the best documentary Oscar
item.description: Mr Nobody Against Putin, a primary school teacher’s record of the indoctrination of his pupils to support Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, has won the Oscar for best documentary.

item.title: One-off £2,000 grant gives care leavers head start, study finds
item.description: Care leavers who were given a one-off, no strings attached £2,000 grant were less likely to become homeless or spend time in hospital or prison, according to a new study.

item.title: Alex Johnston bedlam delivers one of rugby league’s most unforgettable nights | Jack Snape
item.description: Alex Johnston is adored by all in rugby league, but even so, this was extreme.

item.title: Untagged livestock roaming free continues to raise alarm across WA shires
item.description: A farmer in Western Australia's south has joined growing calls for stronger action to address wandering livestock on rural properties and roads.

item.title: Jannik Sinnner ends wait for title with Indian Wells win over Daniil Medvedev
item.description: Jannik Sinner claimed his first title of the year with victory over Daniil Medvedev in Indian Wells, while Aryna Sabalenka snapped her losing streak against Elena Rybakina in a thrilling women’s final.

item.title: Taxpayer bill for saving Scunthorpe steel furnaces could top £1.5bn by 2028, auditor says
item.description: The cost of keeping the UK’s last remaining blast furnaces going at British Steel’s Scunthorpe plant could exceed £1.5bn by 2028 if it continues at its current rate, according to the government’s spending watchdog.

item.title: Oscars 2026: Winners list in full (updating live)
item.description: Hollywood's best and brightest have gathered to find out which of them will be taking home one of the most coveted awards in the movie industry, the Oscars.

item.title: Oscars 2026 winners list: Michael B Jordan takes Best Actor, One Battle After Another wins Best Picture
item.description: Hollywood's night of night has taken place this evening with One Battle After Another dominating.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' takes best picture. Here's the full list of Oscar winners
item.description: One Battle After Another took home best picture at the 98th Oscars on Sunday night.

item.title: Amy Madigan wins best supporting actress Oscar for Weapons
item.description: Amy Madigan has won the best supporting actress Oscar for Weapons at the 98th Academy Awards,.

item.title: First-round of French local elections sees strong showing for National Rally and LFI
item.description: The first-round of the French municipal elections have seen a strong showing for Marine Le Pen’s far-right the National Rally (RN), as well as for Jean-Luc Mélenchon’s radical left, with both parties likely to increase their local presence ahead of next year’s French presidential race.

item.title: Live: Oscars underway as Amy Madigan and KPop Demon Hunters win early
item.description: The 98th Academy Awards are here! Australia's Rose Byrne and Jacob Elordi are in the running to win their first Oscars and potentially cap off an already successful awards season with the top honours.

item.title: Oscars 2026: the red carpet, the ceremony, the winners – follow the action live!

item.title: How this Sydney high school drastically improved their HSC results
item.description: When Janice Neilsen joined South Sydney High School as principal seven years ago, turning around their HSC results was a top priority.

item.title: 'The country is watching': Bernardi says One Nation must win seats in SA poll
item.description: One Nation's performance in recent polling has been one of the prominent themes of the SA election campaign.

item.title: 'A dream come true': What life is like a year after The Piano
item.description: For 14-year-old Melburnian Stefania Robu, walking down the street has become a "bizarre experience".

item.title: Chelsea’s James leads way to win League Cup final against Manchester United
item.description: Death, taxes and Chelsea women winning trophies.

item.title: Manchester United sink Aston Villa to tighten grip on Champions League spot
item.description: On 71 minutes a classic Manchester United riposte, via Matheus Cunha, to Ross Barkley’s equaliser moments before.

item.title: UK looking at all options to secure Strait of Hormuz, says Miliband
item.description: The UK is looking at "any options" - including working with the US and allies - to secure key oil shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz, Ed Miliband has said.

item.title: How a ‘vacuum cleaner turned the other way’ became a popular solution to snoring disorders
item.description: When Nick went camping in the summer with friends, he would set up his tent 100 metres away from the group.

item.title: Grounds for optimism at North Melbourne as emerging talents give glimpse of rosy future | Jonathan Horn
item.description: For most of his tenure at North Melbourne, Alastair Clarkson’s eyebrows have been arched in a kind of perma-frown.

item.title: Prue Leith looks back: ‘I had a great time on Bake Off, but I don’t think I’ll have any yearning when I see Nigella in that position’
item.description: Born in Cape Town in 1940, Prue Leith is a restaurateur, chef, broadcaster and writer.
